content: I'm so stressed about this upcoming family reunion in Park City. My parents are expecting me to bring someone and I'm still single.
Family reunions can definitely be stressful! Have you thought about how you want to handle the 'plus one' situation?
I'm considering just going solo and dealing with the questions. It's not like I haven't been busy - finishing my MBA while working and producing my true crime podcast keeps me pretty occupied.
That's a lot on your plate! Your podcast must be doing well if you're juggling all that. What's your latest episode about?
It's about a cold case from the 90s in Moab. Really fascinating stuff, but it's eaten up all my free time. I haven't even been able to keep up with my calligraphy hobby lately.
Moab sounds intriguing! And I didn't know you did calligraphy - that's such a beautiful art form. Maybe you could incorporate that into your podcast somehow?
I've thought about it - maybe doing lettering for episode titles or merch. Hey, do you know any good hiking trails near Park City? I'm trying to convince my parents I'm not a complete hermit.
Park City has amazing trails! The Armstrong Trail is a great moderate hike with beautiful views. Since you're into tennis too, there are some great courts in town if you want to stay active.
Perfect, I'll check those out. I've been trying to get back into tennis since my schedule calmed down a bit. Maybe I can play with my dad while I'm there.
That sounds like a great bonding activity! How's the rest of your family - are they all in Utah?
Most of them are around Salt Lake. My sister just moved to Denver though, which makes family gatherings smaller. She's the one who got me into true crime podcasts in the first place.
Oh interesting! So you both share that interest. Does she do something similar or is it just a hobby for her?
She's actually a forensic accountant, so she's always pointing out the financial angles in cases. Hey, I should probably start packing for Park City. Thanks for helping me think through this!
Anytime! Enjoy the reunion and maybe bring some of your calligraphy supplies - it could be a fun conversation starter with relatives!
